tierlist.disclaimer_title: Tier lists are guidance, not truth
tierlist.disclaimer_body: Tier lists are not 100% true and depend on multiple factors and each person can have its own opinions. Stake, deck, skill, RNG and playstyle all shift placements. Use this as a starting point, not a rule.
tierlist.empty_tier: No jokers in this tier for this variant.
tierlist.disclaimer_footer: Tier lists on this page are aggregated from public community sources (Reddit threads, YouTube guides, balatrocalc, etc.) and reflect community consensus, not an official Balatro ranking. Sources are listed above; rankings change as the meta evolves.

whatsnew.heading: What's New

whatsnew.footer: Have feedback? The repo is on GitHub. Issues and PRs welcome.

v1_7_4.title: v1.7.4 - Even faster Finder + signed-in fix
v1_7_4.signed_in: Fixed signed-in user button overflowing the sidebar with long email addresses. Now shows 'Signed in'; the full email is on hover and in the dropdown.
v1_7_4.faster: Seed Finder another ~20-40% faster: constraint vectors are now cached per worker instead of re-allocated every batch (no more embind heap churn), result marshaling is skipped on no-match batches, and batch size went 20k to 50k for fewer round-trips.

v1_7_3.title: v1.7.3 - Faster Finder + Home polish
v1_7_3.faster: Seed Finder is ~2-4x faster: doubled the worker thread cap (8 to 16) so all your CPU cores get used, and quadrupled per-batch tries (5k to 20k) to cut postMessage overhead.
v1_7_3.maxante: Removed the redundant global Max ante field. Each joker constraint already has its own max ante; the engine now derives the global cap from those automatically.
v1_7_3.workers: Renamed Threads to CPU workers with a clearer tooltip explaining what they do and that higher = faster.
v1_7_3.home: Fixed desktop home screen being cut off vertically. The animated background and joker conveyor bands now fill the full viewport.

v1_7_2.title: v1.7.2 - UX polish + Seed Library
v1_7_2.persist: Seeds tab now remembers state across navigation: Seed Finder progress, Analyzer view, current seed and results all survive tab switches.
v1_7_2.library: New Seed Library sub-tab. Save any seed from the Finder with one click; the full preset (deck, stake, version, constraints) is saved alongside the seed so you can reload it later.
v1_7_2.soul: Soul resolution: when The Soul appears in an analyzed seed, the resolved Legendary joker and its edition are shown inline, both in the Full Spoiler and the Soul view.
v1_7_2.spoiler: Full Spoiler is now a collapsible per-ante list. Cleaner ante cards with less clutter; click an ante to expand its full breakdown.
v1_7_2.wikilinks: Wikipedia-style internal links across the app: clicking any joker icon or name opens its detail card. Works in synergies, combos, shop queues, pack contents, search results, and Seed Finder matches.
v1_7_2.click_sfx: Mobile fix: click sound effects now only fire on actual button taps, no longer on swipe/scroll gestures.

v1_7.title: v1.7 - Native seed finder
v1_7.rework: Seeds tab fully reworked. Two clean sub-tabs only: Seed Analyzer (input a seed, see everything in it) and Seed Finder (input what you want, find a matching seed). Joker Hunter and Soul Finder are now views inside the Analyzer.
v1_7.finder: Seed Finder uses an autocomplete search bar (no more icon wall): type a joker name, add it as a constraint, repeat for as many jokers as you want.
v1_7.wasm: WebAssembly engine compiled from the same C++ math as TheSoul/Immolate, running in parallel Web Workers across all your CPU cores. Tens of thousands of seeds per second per core.
v1_7.constraints: Multi-joker constraints with edition and source pinning: require a Negative Perkeo specifically from a Spectral pack's Soul card, or a Polychrome Blueprint from any shop, or both at once.
v1_7.location: Locations are now human-readable: 'After Big Blind, shop item 3' or 'After Small Blind, 2nd booster (Mega Buffoon Pack), card #4'. Same labels in the Analyzer spoiler and Joker Finder.
v1_7.icons: Joker icons everywhere on the Seeds tab: shop queue, booster contents, search results, finder matches.

v1_6.title: v1.6 - Native seed engine
v1_6.native_engine: Seeds tab rebuilt from scratch. Native port of Immolate / TheSoul: deterministic per-ante shop queue, packs, vouchers, bosses, tags, and pack contents - all computed in-browser from a seed. No external links.
v1_6.spoiler: Spoiler sub-tab: per-ante breakdown with boss, voucher, both tags, 15-item shop queue, and every pack with its contents (jokers, tarots, planets, spectrals, standard cards with enhancements/editions/seals).
v1_6.hunter: Joker Hunter sub-tab: search any joker by name (Perkeo, Blueprint, Brainstorm, etc.) and see every ante, source (shop or Buffoon pack), rarity, edition, and stickers it appears at.
v1_6.soul_finder: Soul Finder sub-tab: lists every The Soul + Black Hole spawn across all antes, with the pack name and source (Tarot, Spectral, or Celestial pack).
v1_6.verified: Engine cross-verified against TheSoul live for seed 8Q47WV6K (Red, 1.0.1c) - boss, voucher, tags, shop queue, and all pack contents match exactly across antes 1-3.

v1_8.title: v1.8 — Polish pass
v1_8.uiscale: Settings now has a UI scale slider (80% to 160%). Joker sprites and key elements rescale together without breaking layout, on desktop and mobile.

gloss.rarity_def: Jokers come in four rarities; Common, Uncommon, Rare, Legendary. Higher rarities appear less often in the shop and have stronger or more complex effects. Legendaries can only appear from The Soul card.

flush.wants: Five-of-a-suit consistency every hand. Levels Flush hand high and stacks suit payoffs.
flush.oftenLacks: Raw XMult without Idol. Add Cavendish or Blueprint+Idol to unlock late-game ceiling.

straight.wants: Run consistency. Discard-fueled hand-finding plus rank-spanning payoffs.
straight.oftenLacks: Top-end XMult. Bolt on Cavendish or Driver's License once enhancements are in the deck.

high_card.wants: Single-card scoring with massive flat and X mult on one or two payoff cards.
high_card.oftenLacks: Consistency. Add hand-size or retrigger pieces so the single scored card pays repeatedly.

pair.wants: Reliable two-card payoffs with rank or face-card synergies.
pair.oftenLacks: Hand levels. Use Planets aggressively or pivot to two-pair to scale chip output.

two_pair.wants: Four-card scoring. Pairs well with rank-specific payoffs and discards to assemble both pairs.
two_pair.oftenLacks: Mult scaling. Slot a multiplicative payoff like Idol or Cavendish.

three_of_a_kind.wants: Three matching ranks per hand. Strong with rank-specific multipliers and retriggers.
three_of_a_kind.oftenLacks: Setup speed. Often slow to hit consistently without DNA-style duplication.

four_of_a_kind.wants: Big payoff hand with rank density. Combines best with rank-targeted Jokers.
four_of_a_kind.oftenLacks: Reliability; hard to assemble naturally. DNA + hand-size makes it sustainable.

face_card.wants: Face-card density that synergizes with payoff and retrigger Jokers.
face_card.oftenLacks: Chips. Add Stone Joker or a +Chips Joker so face-card mult has something to multiply.

held_in_hand.wants: Cards held during scoring trigger held-in-hand bonuses. Hand size is the resource.
held_in_hand.oftenLacks: Hand size. Aggressively buy hand-size vouchers and avoid hand-size penalties.

steel.wants: Many Steel cards in deck so X1.5 per Steel held-in-hand stacks up.
steel.oftenLacks: Tarot access. Without consistent Tarots, Steel count plateaus too low.

glass.wants: Crack Glass cards to permanently grow Glass Joker. High-variance scaling.
glass.oftenLacks: Defensive stability. Run consistency pieces so shattered scoring cards don't tank a round.

discard.wants: More discards to find hands and to monetize discards as economy.
discard.oftenLacks: Damage payoff. Pure discard is economy; pair with an XMult engine to actually clear blinds.

deck_growth.wants: Permanently add cards (often enhanced) to grow scaling Jokers.
deck_growth.oftenLacks: Tempo. Early antes feel slow; commit only when economy supports buying every pack.

economy_snowball.wants: Maximize income to flood the shop with Jokers and upgrades.
economy_snowball.oftenLacks: Damage payoff. Slot one premium XMult Joker (Cavendish, Triboulet) once interest is capped.

retrigger_engine.wants: Multiple retrigger sources stacked so each scored card fires 2-3 times.
retrigger_engine.oftenLacks: Base payoff. Retriggers multiply existing effects; without a real payoff, you're tripling zero.

face_card_engine.title: Face Card Mult Engine
conditions: Face-card density of 30%+ in deck
Tarots used to convert low cards into face cards
risks: Slow to come online before all three core pieces land
Bricks if Triboulet never appears
face_card_engine.why: Pareidolia makes every scored card a face. Sock & Buskin re-triggers each one. Triboulet applies X2 per face-card trigger. The result is multiplicative scaling that scales linearly with hand size.
face_card_engine.pivotOut: If Triboulet hasn't appeared by Ante 5, pivot into a flat-mult + XMult shell (Cavendish, Blueprint) and treat the face-card pieces as filler.

held_in_hand_steel.title: Baron Steel Held-in-Hand
conditions: Hand-size buffs (Stuntman avoided)
Steel-enhancement tarots accessible
risks: Slow scaling without Tarot access
Steel Joker fizzles if shop denies enhancements
held_in_hand_steel.why: Mime doubles Baron's X1.5 per held King and Steel cards' X1.5; every held King and Steel card pays out twice during the held-in-hand phase, stacking multiplicatively.
held_in_hand_steel.pivotOut: Drop Steel Joker if enhancements never show; keep Baron + Mime + Raised Fist as a pure held-in-hand line.

retrigger_idol.title: Idol Retrigger Burst
conditions: Pick Idol target you can find (face card + common suit)
Lead the chosen card first when scoring
risks: Idol target may not be a face card; pick a King or Queen of the same suit
Single-card dependency
retrigger_idol.why: Hanging Chad triggers the lead card three times. If the lead is Idol's target, that's three X2s; and Sock doubles again when face. One card carries the whole hand.
retrigger_idol.pivotOut: If you can't find a face-card Idol target, switch to a flat-mult shell built around Fibonacci.

flush_mono.title: Mono-Suit Flush Stack
conditions: Two suits funneled into one via Smeared
Flush hand levelled 3+
risks: Without Smeared, suit conflicts cap flush reliability
Idol target rank must overlap with deck
flush_mono.why: Smeared cuts your effective suit count in half. Greedy and Wrathful gain +4 Mult per card of the merged suit, and Idol fires on every match. Flushes become five-card multiplier dumps.
flush_mono.pivotOut: If Smeared never drops, accept inconsistent flushes and pivot toward Fibonacci high-card scaling.

straight_train.title: Straight Through Discards
conditions: Straight hand levelled
Discards available to find the straight
risks: Green Joker conflicts with discard reliance
Fibonacci covers low/mid cards but not 6, 7, J, Q, K
straight_train.why: Drunkard gives extra discards to assemble straights. Fibonacci pays +8 Mult per scored Ace/2/3/5/8. Hack doubles 2/3/4/5 triggers, so Fibonacci's payoff fires twice on overlapping ranks.
straight_train.pivotOut: If straights stop showing, redirect Fibonacci into a high-card or two-pair line where its rank coverage is still relevant.

discard_economy.title: Discard Income Snowball
conditions: Pick a Mail-in Rebate rank you draw often
Avoid Green Joker
risks: Pure economy; needs a damage shell beside it
Boss blinds that block discards crush this line
discard_economy.why: Three independent income hooks fire every round: end-of-round interest, discard-based rebates, and face-card discard bonuses. Compounds via interest cap into runaway shop power.
discard_economy.pivotOut: When interest is capped and shop is built out, drop economy pieces for late-game XMult payoffs.

scaling_snowball.title: Hologram Deck Growth
conditions: Open every Standard pack
Single-play first hand for DNA
risks: Slow start; needs many rounds to compound
Bricks if Standard packs never appear
scaling_snowball.why: Every card added to the deck pumps Hologram. DNA duplicates a card each round. Constellation scales off Planet usage. All three quietly accumulate XMult with no per-hand effort.
scaling_snowball.pivotOut: If pack flow is poor, sell Hologram and consolidate into a payoff shell (Cavendish + Blueprint).

retrigger_engine_general.title: Generic Retrigger Engine
conditions: Some flat or X payoff Joker to amplify
Mix of scored and held-in-hand phases
risks: Without a real payoff, retriggers triple nothing
Stuntman's hand-size cut hurts Mime
retrigger_engine_general.why: Three retrigger sources cover all three phases; scored face cards (Sock), lead scored card (Chad), held-in-hand (Mime). Slot any payoff Joker and it fires 2-3× per round.
retrigger_engine_general.pivotOut: If no payoff Joker appears by Ante 6, drop Mime and pick a static XMult Joker like Cavendish as a safety net.

glass_loop.title: Glass Joker Crack Loop
conditions: Glass enhancements applied via Tarots
Run defensive shell to survive variance
risks: Glass cards permanently lost when they crack
Tail risk of bricking critical scoring cards
glass_loop.why: Hack retriggers low cards twice, doubling Glass shatter chance per scoring round. Each shatter grants permanent +X0.75 Mult to Glass Joker. Long-term, the line outscales most XMult competitors.
glass_loop.pivotOut: If Glass shards run out or scoring becomes too unreliable, pivot to a deck-growth line where deck attrition isn't a downside.

high_card_fib.title: Fibonacci High Card
conditions: Avoid face cards in scoring
High-card hand levelled
risks: Ride the Bus resets on any face card
Caps lower than face-card engines late
high_card_fib.why: All three Jokers reward staying off face cards. Fibonacci pays flat mult on common low ranks, Green Joker grows per hand, Ride the Bus grows per face-less hand. Triple scaling from clean discipline.
high_card_fib.pivotOut: If you accidentally need face cards to clear a blind, drop Ride the Bus and accept the consistency hit.

economy_planet.title: Planet Economy
conditions: Money available to buy every Planet
Hand-level scaling matters more than per-card Mult
risks: No damage payoff on its own
Requires shop luck for Planet density
economy_planet.why: Planet Merchant doubles Planet rates. Satellite pays $1 per unique Planet used. Constellation gains X0.1 per Planet used. One axis pays you, levels your hands, and scales XMult; three returns on every Planet bought.
economy_planet.pivotOut: Once Constellation is large enough to carry rounds alone, sell Satellite and Planet Merchant for premium late-game Jokers.

stone_chip_wall.title: Stone Wall Chips
conditions: Convert junk cards into Stones via Tarots
Patch hand size if pairing with held-in-hand
risks: Stuntman + Mime/Baron is a trap
Chips alone insufficient at high antes
stone_chip_wall.why: Stone Joker turns each Stone card into +25 chips per scored hand. Stuntman gives +250 chips floor. Combined chip totals comfortably hit blind requirements while any XMult Joker carries the mult side.
stone_chip_wall.pivotOut: Sell Stuntman the moment you commit to a held-in-hand build; keep Stone Joker as a chip wall.

economy_pure.title: Interest Snowball
conditions: Hold $30+ cash reserve before each blind
Buy every 9 you see in Standard packs to grow Cloud 9
Defeat Boss Blinds consistently to ramp Rocket payout
Maintain interest cap via To the Moon (+$1 per $5)
risks: Pure economy generates no damage until a payoff Joker is slotted
Bootstraps/Bull only deliver value if cash stays unspent
Boss Blinds that force spending kill Rocket ramp timing
economy_pure.why: Cloud 9 pays per 9 in the deck, Rocket grows per Boss Blind defeated, and To the Moon breaks the normal interest ceiling. Bull converts the held cash into chips each hand while Bootstraps handles flat Mult from the same reserve. All five income hooks are passive and compound; by Ante 6 the build runs $20-$30 per round in passive income while Bull and Bootstraps jointly cover both scoring axes from the same wallet.
economy_pure.pivotOut: Once interest is capped and shop is saturated, sell Cloud 9 or Rocket for a premium late-game XMult Joker like Cavendish or Triboulet.

bootstraps_broke.title: Broke Bootstraps
conditions: Spend down to $4 before each hand to trigger Vagabond Tarot generation
Keep discard count at 0 for Mystic Summit +15 Mult
Let Banner's chip bonus from unused discards stack with Mystic Summit
risks: Requires staying near-broke intentionally, limiting shop options
Vagabond Tarot generation only fires when ≤$4; mismanaging cash blocks it
Mystic Summit and discard-economy Jokers are mutually exclusive
bootstraps_broke.why: Bootstraps provides +2 Mult per $5 held, but Vagabond forces you toward broke; the tension resolves by cycling: spend to ≤$4 to trigger Vagabond's free Tarot, then use that Tarot for enhancement or Planet value before the end of round. Mystic Summit rewards reaching 0 discards. Banner rewards holding discards. Choose one discard axis per run; zero-discard lines stack both Mystic Summit and Banner's held-discard bonus simultaneously.
bootstraps_broke.pivotOut: If Vagabond never appears, replace it with Satellite or Golden Joker and run a standard economy shell with Bootstraps and Bull.

hand_only_discipline.title: Iron Fist (No Discard)
conditions: Never use any discards; Burglar eliminates them, ensuring Mystic Summit always fires
Ramen starts at X2 and never decays when discards are 0
Banner gives +30 Chips per discard remaining; Burglar's +3 hands keeps Banner pool full
risks: No discard access means hand consistency depends entirely on draw luck and hand size
Burglar's −1 hand count per blind conflicts with high-hand-count strategies
Ramen's X2 floor is powerful early but does not scale late; needs another XMult piece
hand_only_discipline.why: Burglar converts discards into 3 extra hands each blind, ensuring you always have plays available without ever discarding. Mystic Summit's +15 Mult fires every hand since discards are always 0. Ramen never decays since no discards occur. Banner collects chips from the 3-discard-equivalent the game credits at blind start. Green Joker accrues +1 Mult per hand played and never loses value. Together the build scores through a clean, high-hand-count loop with no discard-economy conflicts.
hand_only_discipline.pivotOut: If Ramen falls below X1.5 from accidental discards, sell it and slot Cavendish or The Trio for a static XMult floor.

joker_stencil_empty.title: Lean Stencil Build
conditions: Keep total Joker count at 3 (Stencil + 2 others) for X2 Stencil minimum
High sell-value Jokers (Egg, Gift Card accumulation) inflate Swashbuckler
Brainstorm copies the leftmost Joker for free XMult duplication
risks: Stencil rewards fewer Jokers; every additional slot purchased reduces XMult
Madness randomly destroys Jokers, potentially hitting Stencil itself
Swashbuckler's Mult depends on held Joker sell value; selling them for cash drops Mult
joker_stencil_empty.why: Joker Stencil gives X1 Mult per empty Joker slot including itself; a 3-Joker setup with 2 empty slots yields X2. Swashbuckler converts the sell value of the two held Jokers (Stencil + Brainstorm) into flat Mult. Brainstorm copies Stencil for a second X2, producing X4 total from a lean 3-Joker lineup. Egg's growing sell value inflates Swashbuckler passively even while sitting unused.
joker_stencil_empty.pivotOut: Once you've found a premium second Joker (Cavendish, Triboulet), add it and accept the Stencil decay; the net scoring gain from the new Joker exceeds the lost XMult.

pair_specialist.title: Pair Payoff Engine
conditions: Fill deck with high face-card density (Kings, Queens) for Photograph + The Duo overlap
Lead the face card as the first scored card for Hanging Chad + Photograph triple proc
DNA can duplicate the Pair target rank for near-guaranteed Pair hands
risks: Photograph only fires once per hand on the first face card; sort hand carefully
Sly and Jolly are flat Mult/chips only; without The Duo's X2 the ceiling is low
Spare Trousers requires Two Pair, not plain Pair; runs on a distinct hand type
pair_specialist.why: The Duo supplies X2 on any Pair-containing hand; nearly always live. Jolly Joker adds +8 Mult and Sly Joker adds +50 Chips on the same trigger condition. Photograph applies X2 to the first face card, so a King-pair leads with Hanging Chad for three X2 procs from a single card before The Duo fires. The Duo + Photograph alone produces X4 from one Pair hand with a face-card lead.
pair_specialist.pivotOut: If face-card density is insufficient for Photograph, replace it with The Tribe and transition to a Flush-Pair hybrid using Four Fingers.

three_of_a_kind_specialist.title: The Trio Engine
conditions: Use DNA to stack a single rank to 8+ copies for guaranteed Three of a Kind
The Idol target rank must overlap with the duplicated rank for X2 per matched card
Level Three of a Kind hand via Planet cards; high base chips make Wily's +100 decisive
risks: Without DNA, Three of a Kind is inconsistent in a 52-card deck
Full House satisfies The Trio but dilutes DNA-targeting strategy
The Idol's rank+suit rotates; it won't always align with the tripled rank
three_of_a_kind_specialist.why: The Trio delivers X3 on Three of a Kind and Full House; firing alongside Zany Joker's +12 Mult and Wily Joker's +100 Chips creates a comprehensive three-axis scoring package from one hand type. DNA ensures the chosen rank floods the deck, making Three of a Kind trivially consistent. The Idol stacked on top converts each matched card into additional X2 procs, and Blueprint next to The Trio doubles the X3 to X9 when positioned correctly.
three_of_a_kind_specialist.pivotOut: If DNA is unavailable, replace Wily Joker with Hanging Chad and pivot to a face-card Pair build where the rank overlap requirement is less rigid.

straight_flush_endgame.title: Straight Flush Endgame
conditions: Four Fingers + Shortcut reduces Straight Flush to any 4 roughly-sequential cards
Smeared Joker collapses the suit requirement to 2 effective suits
Séance generates a Spectral card each Straight Flush; use for Wraith/Ankh copies
Level Straight Flush hand via Celestial Packs
risks: Requires three specific enabler Jokers before the engine is live
Séance generates random Spectrals; may not always hit Wraith or useful cards
The Order and The Tribe both require their respective hand types, but Straight Flush satisfies both
straight_flush_endgame.why: Four Fingers + Shortcut converts Straight Flush from a rare lucky hand into a near-guaranteed 4-card play. Smeared Joker doubles the card availability by merging suits. Séance turns every Straight Flush into a free Spectral; over a full run this generates 5-10+ powerful Spectrals. The Order (X3) and The Tribe (X2) both fire on Straight Flush simultaneously for a free X6 base XMult before any other Jokers contribute.
straight_flush_endgame.pivotOut: If Séance never appears, use the enabler trio (Four Fingers + Shortcut + Smeared) to run a pure Flush build with The Tribe and Ancient Joker instead.

steel_marble_chip_wall.title: Steel-Marble Wall
conditions: Marble Joker adds Stone cards passively each blind; aim for 10+ Stones for Stone Joker
Steel cards applied via Tarots to held cards; Steel Joker counts them all
Stuntman provides +250 Chips floor; avoid Mime/Baron pairings (hand size conflict)
Driver's License unlocks X3 once 16+ enhanced cards are in the deck
risks: Stuntman reduces hand size by 2, conflicting with held-in-hand pieces like Mime
Marble Joker grows deck size, increasing draw variance
Driver's License requires 16 enhanced cards; slow to enable without Tarot density
steel_marble_chip_wall.why: Marble Joker passively builds Stone count toward Stone Joker's chip scaling, while Steel cards added via Tarots grow Steel Joker's XMult. Stuntman adds a +250 chip floor. Driver's License flips on when both Stone and Steel enhancements reach the 16-card threshold. The build needs no per-hand conditions; it accumulates chips and XMult passively across blinds while Tarots convert the deck.
steel_marble_chip_wall.pivotOut: If Tarot access is poor and Driver's License threshold is unreachable, sell Stuntman and focus purely on Stone Joker + Marble Joker + Blueprint for a chip-dominant but simpler line.

sixth_sense_spectral_loop.title: Spectral Economy Loop
conditions: Lead each round with a single 6 play for Sixth Sense's Spectral generation
Cartomancer creates a Tarot every blind start; keep a consumable slot open
Perkeo duplicates a consumable each shop; hold Tarots/Planets across the shop
Certificate adds a sealed card each round; Red Seal retriggers, Gold Seal earns money
risks: Requires tight consumable slot management; at most 2 slots available
Sixth Sense needs a 6 available in hand as the first play every round
Fortune Teller's Mult scales only with Tarots used, not Spectrals
sixth_sense_spectral_loop.why: Sixth Sense generates one Spectral per round by destroying a 6, simultaneously thinning the deck. Cartomancer adds a Tarot every blind entry. Perkeo duplicates a random consumable at shop end, turning one Tarot or Planet into two. Certificate provides a free sealed card each round. Over a run, this generates 20+ consumables; enough to fully enhance the deck, max Fortune Teller's Mult, and repeatedly use Planets to feed Constellation.
sixth_sense_spectral_loop.pivotOut: If Sixth Sense is unavailable, replace it with Hallucination (50% Tarot on every pack opened) and run a Cartomancer + Perkeo + Fortune Teller triple-Tarot line.

wee_joker_scaling.title: Wee Joker Chip Engine
conditions: Maximise 2s in the deck; DNA duplication of 2s is ideal
Hack retriggers each played 2, doubling Wee Joker growth to +16 Chips per 2
Seltzer active window doubles all card triggers for 10 hands; maximise 2s during Seltzer
Blueprint copies Wee Joker, doubling chip accumulation speed
risks: Wee Joker is chip-scaling only; needs an XMult source to convert large chips into damage
Seltzer is timer-limited (10 hands); time acquisition near boss blinds for maximum value
DNA single-play requirement conflicts with scoring hands; plan the duplication round carefully
wee_joker_scaling.why: Each played 2 permanently adds +8 Chips to Wee Joker. Hack retriggers every 2 once extra, making each 2 add +16 per round. Blueprint copies Wee Joker, doubling the accumulation rate to +32 per 2 per hand. Seltzer's 10-hand window retriggers every card including 2s, compressing months of growth into a short burst. With 4+ 2s in a hand under Seltzer + Hack + Blueprint, Wee Joker can gain hundreds of chips in a single round.
wee_joker_scaling.pivotOut: Once Wee Joker's chip base is large enough to carry blinds on chips alone, sell Seltzer and replace it with Stone Joker or Stuntman to harden the chip foundation.

madness_sacrifice.title: Sacrifice Engine
conditions: Riff-Raff generates 2 Common Jokers each blind; they populate the random Madness sacrifice pool
Ceremonial Dagger positioned left of a Riff-Raff Common feeds on those Commons for double sell-value Mult
Keep total Joker count low (3-4) so Madness sacrifices Riff-Raff Commons, not engines
Campfire grows X0.25 per sold Joker; combine with pre-boss Joker sales for burst
risks: Madness sacrifice is random; Ceremonial Dagger, Blueprint, or Madness itself could be targeted
Riff-Raff Commons need a free Joker slot to spawn; Madness consumption creates this slot naturally
Campfire resets at Boss Blind defeat; timing the pre-boss sale spike is essential
madness_sacrifice.why: Riff-Raff floods the Joker roster with free Commons each blind. Madness consumes a random Joker for X0.5 XMult gain; the Riff-Raff Commons serve as disposable fuel, absorbing Madness's random destruction. Ceremonial Dagger sacrifices the Riff-Raff Common to its right for double-sell-value flat Mult each blind. Together the three form a self-replenishing sacrifice loop: Riff-Raff creates fuel, Ceremonial Dagger burns it for Mult, Madness converts random deaths into XMult stacks.
madness_sacrifice.pivotOut: If Madness destroys a key engine Joker twice, sell Madness and run Ceremonial Dagger + Riff-Raff alone as a stable flat-Mult scaling line.

ancient_joker_suit_rotation.title: Ancient Suit Rotate
conditions: Smeared Joker collapses Ancient's rotating suit from 1-in-4 to 1-in-2 coverage
Four Fingers reduces flush to 4 cards, ensuring scored cards match Ancient's active suit more often
The Idol's rotating target occasionally aligns with Ancient's suit for X1.5 × X2 = X3 per card
Bloodstone adds probabilistic X1.5 on Heart cards; pairs with Smeared's Heart-Diamond merge
risks: Ancient Joker's suit rotates each round; rounds where your deck skews against the active suit lose value
The Idol alignment with Ancient is occasional, not guaranteed
Smeared conflicts with Flower Pot (which needs all four suits); cannot run both
ancient_joker_suit_rotation.why: Ancient Joker applies X1.5 per scored card of the active suit, but the rotating nature punishes mono-suit decks. Smeared Joker collapses four suits to two, guaranteeing roughly 50% of scored cards trigger X1.5 on any given round. Four Fingers increases the average number of scored cards per hand in flush builds, directly multiplying Ancient's output. When The Idol's rotating target matches Ancient's active suit, each matching card hits X1.5 × X2 = X3; a free double-dip that rewards flexible suit coverage.
ancient_joker_suit_rotation.pivotOut: If The Idol's rotation is too unreliable to align with Ancient, replace it with The Tribe for a flat X2 on every Flush hand, keeping the Smeared + Four Fingers consistency core intact.

blueprint__brainstorm.why: Position-locked copy pair. Stack them around your strongest XMult engine to multiply its output three times in the same scoring pass.

blueprint__the_idol.why: Each Idol match is X2; Blueprint doubles the dip on the same scored hand, turning matched cards into X4-per-card scaling.

brainstorm__triboulet.why: Position Triboulet far-left so Brainstorm copies it. Every King and Queen scored applies two stacked X2 multipliers.

baron__mime.why: Baron's X1.5 per held King is re-applied by Mime in the held-in-hand phase, turning each King into X2.25.

mime__steel_joker.why: Steel cards trigger their X1.5 once in held-in-hand. Mime re-triggers, doubling Steel pressure for free.

sock_and_buskin__triboulet.why: Sock retriggers every scored face card, and each retrigger re-applies Triboulet's X2; the canonical face-card scaling loop.

pareidolia__triboulet.why: Pareidolia makes every scored card count as a face card, so Triboulet's X2 fires on the whole hand instead of just Kings and Queens.

midas_mask__pareidolia.why: Midas turns scored face cards into Gold; Pareidolia means every card qualifies, snowballing income each hand.

greedy_joker__smeared_joker.why: Smeared merges Diamonds with Hearts, doubling the count of cards that proc Greedy's +4 Mult.

smeared_joker__the_idol.why: Halves the effective suit pool, so Idol's rank+suit target shows up roughly twice as often per shuffle.

glass_joker__hack.why: Hack retriggers low cards twice, doubling chances of cracking Glass each hand and accelerating Glass Joker's permanent X0.75 stacks.

fibonacci__hack.why: 2, 3, and 5 are both Fibonacci ranks and Hack ranks; every Fibonacci proc fires twice.

mime__raised_fist.why: Mime re-triggers the held-in-hand phase, so Raised Fist's flat mult applies twice from a single low card kept in hand.

hanging_chad__the_idol.why: Lead with the Idol-target card: Hanging Chad triggers it three times, stacking three X2 multiplications from one scored card.

dna__the_idol.why: DNA permanently duplicates whatever you single-play. Pick an Idol-target card and farm its X2 procs by stacking the deck.

dna__hologram.why: Every DNA-cloned card grows Hologram by X0.25; a free XMult scaling loop.

astronomer__constellation.why: Astronomer makes all Planet cards and Celestial Packs free; every free Planet fuels Constellation's X0.1-per-use XMult accumulation; the two convert saved money into free exponential scaling across the run.

astronomer__satellite.why: Satellite scales income with unique Planets used; Planet Merchant guarantees you see more Planet variety.

drunkard__mail_in_rebate.why: Extra discards mean more chances to dump rebate-target ranks for money each round.

drunkard__faceless_joker.why: More discards make hitting the 3-face-card threshold easier, especially in face-heavy decks.

baron__stuntman.why: Stuntman cuts hand size by 2, gutting Baron's per-King multiplier. Only run together if you patch hand size aggressively.

ride_the_bus__triboulet.why: Ride the Bus resets on any scored face card; Triboulet wants to score face cards. They actively cancel each other.

drunkard__green_joker.why: Green Joker loses mult per discard, undoing Drunkard's whole purpose. Pick one direction.

steel_joker__vampire.why: Vampire eats enhancements off your cards. Every card it consumes lowers Steel Joker's permanent stack.

greedy_joker__pareidolia.why: Once everything is a face card, suit-specific Jokers like Greedy stop differentiating their bonus reliably in mixed builds.

constellation__perkeo.why: Perkeo duplicates Planet consumables as Negative copies; Constellation rides the extra Planet plays for free XMult.

seltzer__triboulet.why: Seltzer doubles every scored card for 10 hands. Spike Triboulet during boss blinds, then pivot when it expires.

golden_joker__midas_mask.why: Two stacked income sources; Midas converts face cards to gold while Golden Joker pays passive end-of-round interest support.

driver_gloves__stone_joker.why: Stones count as Enhanced cards toward Driver's License threshold and individually pump Stone Joker chips. Two payoffs from one conversion.

blueprint__obelisk.why: Once Obelisk has parked a huge XMult, Blueprint copies it; but only commit when you've truly stopped playing your most-played hand.

photograph__sock_and_buskin.why: Sock and Buskin retriggers every scored face card; Photograph procs on the FIRST face card; after the retrigger, Photograph's trigger window fires again, delivering two X2 ticks from a single face card.

smiley_face__sock_and_buskin.why: Each retrigger by Sock and Buskin replays the scored face card in full, doubling every per-face-card flat-mult Joker including Smiley Face's +5 Mult; five face cards in a flush become +50 Mult instead of +25.

scary_face__sock_and_buskin.why: Sock and Buskin retriggers every face card; each retrigger also doubles Scary Face's +30 Chips chip bonus, covering the chip side that Sock's face-card mult engine typically lacks.

dusk__the_idol.why: Dusk retriggers every scored card on the final hand of the round; if the Idol-target card is in that hand, each retrigger fires another X2, stacking multiplicatively on a hand already delivering the burst payoff.

acrobat__dusk.why: Both effects trigger on the final hand of the round; Acrobat contributes X3 and Dusk retriggers every played card, letting Acrobat's X3 apply to a doubled scoring pass on the same hand.

hanging_chad__triboulet.why: Hanging Chad triggers the lead scoring card three times total; if that card is a King or Queen, Triboulet applies its X2 three times in sequence; X2×X2×X2 = X8 from one well-placed face card.

hack__wee_joker.why: Hack retriggers every played 2 once extra; each retrigger counts as an additional scored 2, doubling Wee Joker's permanent +8 Chips accumulation per 2 scored; one played 2 yields +16 Chips of permanent growth instead of +8.

even_steven__hack.why: 2 and 4 are both Even Steven ranks and Hack ranks; every played 2 or 4 fires Even Steven's +4 Mult twice, effectively +8 Mult per low even card scored.

seltzer__the_idol.why: Seltzer retriggers every scored card for 10 hands; each retrigger re-fires The Idol's X2 on matching cards, turning a single Idol-target card into X4 per triggered copy for the Seltzer window.

mime__shoot_the_moon.why: Shoot the Moon gives +13 Mult per Queen held in hand during the held-in-hand phase; Mime re-triggers that phase, giving +26 Mult per held Queen for free from a single held-hand phase.

mime__reserved_parking.why: Reserved Parking triggers per held face card in the held-in-hand phase; Mime re-triggers the held-in-hand phase, doubling each face card's 1-in-2 income roll and roughly doubling the passive money earned.

hanging_chad__photograph.why: Photograph fires once per hand on the first face card scored; Hanging Chad retriggers the first scored card two extra times; if the lead card is a face card, Photograph's X2 applies on all three passes.

blueprint__triboulet.why: Blueprint copies the Joker to its right; positioned immediately left of Triboulet it fires Triboulet's X2-per-King/Queen a second time on the same scoring pass, turning each face card into X4 per trigger.

brainstorm__the_idol.why: Position The Idol leftmost; Brainstorm copies it, applying X2 per matching card twice in the same scoring pass; one Idol-target card goes from X2 to X4 at zero extra cost.

blueprint__the_duo.why: The Duo applies X2 whenever a Pair appears in the hand; Blueprint copies it to deliver a second X2 on the same hand, making every Pair-containing hand a free X4 XMult stack.

the_order__the_tribe.why: Both conditional XMult Jokers fire simultaneously on Straight Flush hands; The Tribe contributes X2 and The Order contributes X3, stacking to X6 on every Straight Flush scored.

ancient_joker__smeared_joker.why: Ancient Joker applies X1.5 per scored card of the active suit; Smeared Joker collapses four suits to two, so roughly half your deck triggers X1.5 on any hand regardless of suit rotation.

bloodstone__oops_all_6s.why: Oops! All 6s doubles Bloodstone's 1-in-2 Heart proc to guaranteed; every scored Heart card becomes a certain X1.5 tick, turning Heart flushes into a reliable X1.5-per-card multiplicative engine.

baseball_card__constellation.why: Baseball Card gives X1.5 Mult per Uncommon Joker owned; Constellation is Uncommon and one of the strongest XMult scalers in the game; owning both gives X1.5 from Baseball Card for Constellation's presence, while Constellation independently compounds via Planets.

cavendish__dna.why: DNA single-play duplication can target any card; cloning an Ace into a deck stacked with four copies guarantees consistent high-value hands while Cavendish provides the X3 payoff on every scored hand that emerges.

marble_joker__stone_joker.why: Marble Joker adds one Stone card to the deck every blind; each added Stone grows Stone Joker by +25 Chips per scored hand; passive blind-count scaling delivers a free chip engine with no per-hand effort required.

perkeo__sixth_sense.why: Sixth Sense produces a Spectral card each round by sacrificing a 6; Perkeo duplicates a random consumable each shop; holding the Spectral across shop visits guarantees a free Negative Spectral copy, effectively doubling every Sixth Sense proc over the run.

cartomancer__fortune_teller.why: Cartomancer creates a free Tarot every blind selection; each Tarot used permanently adds +1 Mult to Fortune Teller; the two form a closed loop where Tarot generation directly translates into permanent flat mult.

certificate__steel_joker.why: Certificate adds a sealed random card to your hand each round start; when those added cards carry Purple Seals or are manually enhanced to Steel, Steel Joker's XMult grows for free each round without Tarot expenditure.

erosion__trading_card.why: Trading Card destroys the first single-discard card each round and earns $3; each destroyed card permanently grows Erosion by +4 Mult; free passive mult scaling every round that also provides income.

four_fingers__smeared_joker.why: Four Fingers reduces flush requirement to 4 cards; Smeared Joker halves the suit pool; together nearly any four cards from a two-suit deck form a flush, making 5-card flush builds nearly unconditional.

four_fingers__shortcut.why: Shortcut allows gapped straights; Four Fingers reduces the count to 4; any four roughly sequential cards form a straight, unlocking Straight Flush as a nearly-free hand type when combined with Smeared.

burglar__mystic_summit.why: Burglar eliminates all discards at blind start; Mystic Summit requires exactly 0 remaining discards for its +15 Mult bonus; Burglar guarantees Mystic Summit fires every single hand without any discipline overhead.

card_sharp__loyalty_card.why: Loyalty Card wants you to play every 6th hand big; Card Sharp gives X3 on repeated hand-type plays; they create cycle tension where the X4 Loyalty trigger forces a hand choice that might reset or conflict with Card Sharp's same-hand bonus.

oops_all_6s__space_joker.why: Space Joker's 1-in-4 free hand-level upgrade doubles to 2-in-4 (50%) via Oops! All 6s, turning passive hand-levelling into a near-reliable engine that grows base chips and mult every other hand.

bull__to_the_moon.why: To the Moon raises the effective interest cap, letting you hold more money profitably; Bull converts each held dollar into +2 Chips per scored hand; the same cash reserve that earns more interest also delivers more chips every hand.

cloud_9__rocket.why: Both are end-of-round passive income sources that stack independently; Cloud 9 scales with 9-density while Rocket scales with Boss blinds defeated, covering early and late phases of the run with distinct income curves.

delayed_gratification__golden_joker.why: Delayed Gratification pays $2 per unspent discard if no discards are used; Golden Joker's end-of-round $4 stacks on top; running both in a clean no-discard build generates $4 + (discards × $2) passively every round.

greedy_joker__rough_gem.why: Rough Gem earns $1 for each scored Diamond; Greedy Joker earns +3 Mult per scored Diamond; every Diamond card scored simultaneously advances both income and scoring, a rare dual-payoff per card.

golden_ticket__midas_mask.why: Midas Mask converts scored face cards to Gold permanently; Golden Ticket earns $4 each time a Gold card is scored; once face cards are converted, every face card scored returns $4 on top of its scoring contribution.

business_card__pareidolia.why: Business Card has a 1-in-2 chance to pay $2 per scored face card; Pareidolia makes every card a face card; in a 5-card hand, expected income per hand jumps from 1-2 cards qualifying to all 5, roughly tripling Business Card's income.

gift_card__swashbuckler.why: Gift Card adds $1 of sell value to every Joker each round; Swashbuckler converts total Joker sell value into flat Mult; Gift Card passively inflates Swashbuckler's Mult by $1 per Joker per round, compounding multiplicatively over the run.

perkeo__vagabond.why: Vagabond creates a Tarot whenever you play with ≤$4; Perkeo duplicates a random consumable at end of shop; deliberately spending to the Vagabond threshold before shopping then triggers Perkeo on the generated Tarot, effectively producing two Tarots for the cost of one planned low-balance hand.

pareidolia__sock_and_buskin.why: Pareidolia makes every card a face card; Sock and Buskin retriggers every scored face card; the combination retriggers every single card played in any hand, effectively doubling all per-card scoring contributions.

pareidolia__scary_face.why: With Pareidolia active every scored card qualifies for Scary Face's +30 Chips, turning a 5-card hand into a guaranteed +150 Chips chip floor on top of base hand chips; a consistent chip foundation for any build.

pareidolia__smiley_face.why: Pareidolia makes every card a face card, so Smiley Face's +5 Mult fires on each of the five scored cards; +25 flat Mult baseline on any hand, stacking with suit Jokers and hand-levelling as a reliable floor.

canio__pareidolia.why: Canio gains X1 Mult permanently each time a face card is destroyed; Pareidolia makes every card a face card; any card that gets destroyed (Glass cracking, Sixth Sense consuming a 6, trading card kills) now advances Canio's multiplier, drastically expanding the pool of destruction-eligible cards.

dusk__triboulet.why: Dusk retriggers all scored cards on the final hand; Triboulet fires X2 per King or Queen scored; the final-hand retrigger doubles every Triboulet X2 application, turning a two-King final hand from X4 to X8 at no extra cost.

drunkard__hit_the_road.why: Hit the Road gains X0.5 Mult per discarded Jack each round; Drunkard gives +1 discard per round; the extra discard enables one more Jack discard opportunity, consistently pushing Hit the Road's per-round growth from X1.5 to X2 per 3-Jack deck.

merry_andy__yorick.why: Yorick gains X1 Mult per 23 cards discarded; Merry Andy adds +3 discards per round; the extra discards across a full run compress Yorick's 23-card threshold from ~6 rounds to ~4 rounds, accelerating its permanent XMult stack.

castle__drunkard.why: Castle gains +3 Chips per discarded card of its rotating suit; Drunkard adds +1 discard per round; each extra discard generates an additional chip-scaling opportunity every round if the discarded card matches the active Castle suit.

burnt_joker__faceless_joker.why: Burnt Joker upgrades the discarded poker hand type each round as a free hand-level boost; Faceless Joker pays $5 for discarding 3+ face cards simultaneously; both rewards fire off the same discard action without competing for resources.

hit_the_road__yorick.why: Both Jokers scale their XMult from discard volume; Yorick permanently accumulates across the run while Hit the Road resets each round but pays off quickly within a single round's discard budget, covering both long-game and burst-scaling axes.

mystic_summit__ramen.why: Ramen loses X0.01 Mult per discard and Mystic Summit only activates at zero discards; running both enforces a no-discard discipline that preserves Ramen's X2 cap and guarantees Mystic Summit's +15 Mult fires every hand the round.

hack__vampire.why: Hack retriggers every played 2-5; if any of those low cards are enhanced, each retrigger counts as an additional enhanced card trigger for Vampire's XMult accumulation, doubling Vampire's growth rate on low-rank enhanced cards.

driver_gloves__marble_joker.why: Marble Joker adds a Stone card to the deck each blind; Stone cards count as Enhanced cards toward Driver's License's 16-card threshold; together they passively reach the X3 threshold without any Tarot expenditure.

glass_joker__seltzer.why: Seltzer retriggers every played card for 10 hands; Glass cards have their destruction chance applied on each retrigger; each Seltzer-active hand roughly doubles the number of Glass shatters, accelerating Glass Joker's permanent XMult stack in a concentrated burst.

lucky_cat__oops_all_6s.why: Lucky Cat grows X0.25 Mult per Lucky card proc; Oops! All 6s doubles the 1-in-5 trigger chance to 2-in-5; nearly doubling average Lucky Cat growth per round and converting it from a slow scaler into a mid-game XMult engine.

driver_gloves__steel_joker.why: Steel cards counted by Steel Joker for XMult also count toward Driver's License's 16-enhanced-card threshold; stacking Steel enhancements simultaneously grows the XMult engine and unlocks the flat X3, two payoffs from one enhancement campaign.

smeared_joker__vampire.why: Vampire grows from scored enhanced cards and doesn't care about suit; Smeared Joker has no direct interaction, but in flush builds Smeared increases the number of cards scored per hand, indirectly raising the number of enhanced card triggers per round.

lusty_joker__smeared_joker.why: Smeared Joker merges Hearts with Diamonds; Lusty Joker rewards scored Heart cards; every Diamond in the hand now counts as a Heart, doubling the number of cards that trigger Lusty's +3 Mult in Heart-Diamond flush builds.

gluttonous_joker__smeared_joker.why: Smeared Joker merges Clubs with Spades; Gluttonous Joker rewards Club cards; every Spade card now triggers Gluttonous's +3 Mult, effectively doubling dark-suit payoffs across Spade/Club flush builds.

blackboard__smeared_joker.why: Blackboard gives X3 Mult when all held cards are Spades or Clubs; Smeared merges Spades and Clubs into one effective suit; any hand composed of dark-suit cards now satisfies Blackboard unconditionally.

smeared_joker__wrathful_joker.why: Smeared merges Spades and Clubs; Wrathful rewards scored Spades; every Club now counts as Spade, doubling the number of Wrathful +3 Mult triggers in Club/Spade builds and making Wrathful viable alongside Gluttonous.

flower_pot__four_fingers.why: Flower Pot requires all four suits in one hand for X3; Four Fingers reduces the hand size requirement to 4 cards; a 4-card hand containing one card of each suit satisfies Flower Pot, and the freed fifth slot can hold a key card for another payoff.

ancient_joker__the_idol.why: Ancient Joker rotates its active suit each round; The Idol rotates its target rank+suit each round; when they align on the same suit in the same round, the matching card delivers both X1.5 from Ancient and X2 from Idol for a X3 per-card burst.

hologram__marble_joker.why: Marble Joker adds one Stone card to the deck each blind; every added card grows Hologram by X0.25; without spending a cent or opening packs, Marble drives Hologram's XMult upward by X0.25 every blind.

acrobat__obelisk.why: Obelisk builds XMult by avoiding your most-played hand; Acrobat provides X3 on the final hand per round; in a parked Obelisk build where you always play an off-type final hand, Acrobat's X3 fires exactly when Obelisk's accumulated XMult is highest.

burglar__green_joker.why: Burglar removes all discards at blind start; Green Joker grows per hand and shrinks per discard; Burglar guarantees no discards are ever spent, protecting Green Joker's scaling from erosion while providing extra hands for faster growth.

blueprint__supernova.why: Supernova adds Mult equal to the number of times the current hand type has been played this run; Blueprint copies it, applying that same Mult bonus a second time; late in a specialised run this doubles a potentially massive accumulated count.

joker_stencil__swashbuckler.why: Joker Stencil rewards empty Joker slots with XMult; Swashbuckler converts other Jokers' sell value into flat Mult; the two build from opposite directions and only coexist in a lean high-value Joker build where every slot earns maximum sell value.

madness__riff_raff.why: Madness destroys a random Joker each non-boss blind for X0.5 XMult gain; Riff-Raff generates two Common Jokers every blind; Riff-Raff's output feeds Madness's destruction engine so your valuable Jokers are protected by disposable Commons taking the hit.

joker_stencil__madness.why: Madness destroys Jokers (reducing your count) which increases Joker Stencil's XMult; but Madness picks randomly and could destroy Stencil itself or your other engines; only viable in a tightly controlled 2-Joker setup where you accept the risk.

riff_raff__showman.why: Showman allows duplicate Jokers in the shop; Riff-Raff generates Common Jokers on blind entry; combined with Abstract Joker or Brainstorm, the duplicate-enablement can produce multiple copies of a key Common Joker for a stacking payoff.

credit_card__throwback.why: Throwback gains X0.25 Mult per skipped blind; Credit Card allows going $20 into debt; the debt room lets you buy key pieces without cash, enabling you to skip marginally beneficial blinds and harvest Throwback stacks even on cash-tight early antes.

chips.def: The blue number; flat additive score before mult is applied. Hand types and scored cards contribute chips.

mult.def: The red number; flat additive multiplier. Final score = chips × mult.

xmult.def: A multiplicative multiplier applied to the mult value. Two X2 sources stack as X4 (multiplicatively), not as X3 (additively).

retrigger.def: Cause a card or effect to apply more than once in a single scoring pass. Often the highest-leverage scaling type in the game.

scaling.def: Any source of growth that increases over a run, either per-hand (linear) or compounding (exponential). Engines that scale outpace static Jokers late.

consistency.def: How often a build hits its target hand or condition. Discards, hand size, and deck thinning raise consistency.

economy.def: Money generation. Interest, shop discounts, end-of-round income, and gold cards. Funds upgrades that translate into damage later.

held_in_hand.def: The phase after scoring where cards still in your hand can trigger effects (Baron's Kings, Steel cards' X1.5).

thinning.def: Removing weak or junk cards from your deck to draw your important cards more often. Tarots and Spectrals are the main tools.

pivot.def: Switch your build's primary scaling axis mid-run, usually when a key Joker fails to appear or a better one shows up in the shop.

payoff.def: A Joker that converts an enabler condition into real score (e.g., Triboulet converting face cards into XMult). Payoffs need enablers to function.

enabler.def: A Joker or card that makes a condition reliable (e.g., Pareidolia enabling face-card payoffs on every card). Enablers without payoffs do nothing alone.

tempo.def: How quickly a build comes online relative to ante difficulty. Slow-scaling builds need defensive shells to survive early antes.

about.title: About Balatropedia
about.subtitle: A community-sourced strategy companion for Balatro.
about.what_body: Balatropedia is a fan-made companion for Balatro by LocalThunk and Playstack. It indexes every joker, deck, stake, voucher, boss blind, consumable, edition and enhancement, then adds planning tools: My Run notebook, Score Calculator, Build Lab, Skeleton run planner, Seed Analyzer/Finder/Library, and a community-driven Tier List. It is offered free, has no ads, and stores nothing about you beyond what you choose to sync via Google sign-in (your favorites, saved runs, and custom tier list).
about.accuracy_body: Joker effects, scaling formulas, base values and synergy notes are cross-referenced against the official wiki and the community guides listed below. Numbers are best-effort and can lag patches; if you spot a mistake, please open an issue on GitHub.
about.fandom_attribution: Joker text and base values adapted from the Balatro Wiki under CC-BY-SA 3.0. Strategy commentary is editorial.
about.disclaimer_body: Balatropedia is an unofficial, non-commercial fan project. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by LocalThunk, Playstack, or any joker tier-list author. All Balatro names, art references and gameplay mechanics belong to their respective owners and are used here under fair-use for reference and educational purposes. Joker sprites are reduced-scale identifiers used to help players navigate the reference; if a rights holder requests removal of any asset, contact us via the GitHub issue tracker and it will be removed promptly. Community tier-list orderings reflect aggregated opinions from cited third-party guides and are not authoritative; the in-app tier list lets you drag jokers freely to encode your own view.
about.fanmade: A fan-made, non-commercial project. Balatro © LocalThunk & Playstack.

help.title: Help
help.subtitle: What every tab does, how to use it, and tips.

overview.title: Overview
overview.body:
Balatropedia is a fan-made companion for Balatro. It indexes every joker, deck, stake, voucher, boss blind, consumable, and gameplay modifier, then layers planning and analysis tools on top.

The left sidebar groups tabs by purpose. Reference tabs (Jokers, Decks, Bosses, etc.) are pure lookup. Run tabs (My Run, Compare, Skeleton, Run Challenge) help you plan an active or hypothetical run. Tools (Build Lab, Score Calculator, Seeds) crunch numbers and discover content. Tier List and Synergies summarize community consensus.
overview.tip: Star anything to add it to Favorites. Sign in (Google) to sync favorites, saved runs, build lab states, and tier list edits across devices.

library.title: Jokers Library
library.body:
The Jokers tab is the master index. Filter by rarity, edition, scaling type, hand affinity, suit, archetype, or full-text search across names and effects.

Click any card to open a detail sheet with: base effect, scaling formula when applicable, popularity rating, difficulty, recommended decks/stakes, synergies, counter jokers, and curated source links.
library.tip: Use the search bar with effect words ('retrigger', 'mult', 'discard') to find every joker that touches a mechanic.

myrun.title: My Run
myrun.body:
My Run is your live notebook for an active Balatro session. Paste your seed, pick your deck, stake, ante, and current jokers, then the tab surfaces relevant synergies, counter blinds, and shop priorities for your build.

State persists locally and syncs to your Google account when signed in, so closing the tab does not wipe your run.
myrun.tip: Pair My Run with the Seeds tab once a seed is loaded: shop predictions and pack contents stay consistent with your engine.

runchallenge.title: Run Challenge
runchallenge.body:
A daily and on-demand challenge generator. Picks a deck, stake, ante target, and a forced joker pool to constrain your run. Useful for breaking habits and forcing unfamiliar builds.

Each challenge displays the seed (when applicable), constraints, and a difficulty estimate based on the joker pool quality.
runchallenge.tip: Re-roll the challenge if it asks for a joker pool you cannot reasonably build around; the generator is deliberately spicy.

buildlab.title: Build Lab
buildlab.body:
The Build Lab is a sandbox for theorycrafting a 5-joker board. Pick jokers, editions, and the active hand, and the lab computes chip and mult contributions plus the final score for that hand.

It is not a perfect simulator (some scoring quirks are approximated), but it is fast enough to compare two builds side by side.
buildlab.tip: Use it to test 'should I keep X or swap for Y' decisions; the delta column shows score impact directly.

calculator.title: Score Calculator
calculator.body:
A focused chip/mult/X-mult calculator. Enter the played hand, hand level, base chips and mult, then layer modifiers from jokers, editions, and enhancements. The breakdown view shows the order of operations Balatro actually uses.

Good for understanding why a hand 'should' have scored more, or proving a counter-intuitive multiplier ordering.
calculator.tip: Toggle 'X-mult last' to see how negative jokers and joker order change the final number.

seeds.title: Seeds (Analyzer · Finder · Library)
seeds.body:
The Seeds tab has three sub-tabs.

Analyzer: paste a seed, configure deck and stake, and the WASM engine simulates antes ahead of time, showing shop slots, packs, vouchers, and Soul resolutions per blind.

Finder: search the entire seed space for a seed matching your constraints (specific joker in ante 1, a guaranteed Negative tag, etc.). Adjust CPU workers and tries per batch to trade speed against responsiveness.

Library: curated and user-saved notable seeds, browsable by tag.
seeds.tip: If Finder is slow, lower CPU workers to free your machine; if it's idle, push them higher. State persists between sessions.

synergies.title: Synergies
synergies.body:
A directed graph of joker pairings drawn from community guides (dood.gg, Balatro HQ, Mobalytics, validated Reddit threads). Each link is annotated with why it works, the engine type (XMult stack, deck manipulation, scaling, consistency), popularity, and difficulty.

Switch between A and B sides to see what jokers pair with a given target.
synergies.tip: Sort by engine type to find every XMult stacker or every retrigger enabler at a glance.

combos.title: Combos
combos.body:
Three-plus joker combos that escalate beyond simple pairs. Each combo lists the trigger sequence, win-rate notes when known, the deck/stake that enables it, and source guides.

Combos are heavier commitments than synergies and usually require deck preparation across multiple antes.
combos.tip: Filter by 'staple' popularity for the safest combos; 'experimental' is for late-stake stunts.

archetypes.title: Archetypes
archetypes.body:
Build archetypes group jokers by the role they fill in a run: scaling backbone, XMult finisher, retrigger enabler, economy, deck control, etc.

Use this tab to plan a build around a role rather than a single joker, and to see the substitution graph when your ideal pick does not appear in shop.
archetypes.tip: Each archetype lists 'when to commit' notes so you do not pivot too late.

tierlist.title: Tier List
tierlist.body:
An interactive S/A/B/C/D ranking of every joker. The default ordering reflects community consensus aggregated from Mobalytics, TheGamer, and Reddit threads; it is not authoritative.

You can drag jokers between tiers locally; when signed in, your custom tier list syncs to your account. The disclaimer at the bottom of the tab explains the sourcing.
tierlist.tip: Right-click a joker (or long-press on mobile) to open its detail sheet without leaving the tier list.

compare.title: Compare
compare.body: Side-by-side compare for any two jokers, decks, vouchers, or bosses. Effects, scaling, sources, popularity, and synergies appear in parallel columns. Useful when shop hands you a forced choice between two cards.
compare.tip: The 'delta' row highlights only the differences; ignore matching rows to decide quickly.

skeleton.title: Skeleton
skeleton.body:
Skeleton is a 5-slot run planner: pick a deck, stake, and target build, then commit jokers ante by ante to test whether the plan survives boss blind constraints.

It does not simulate scoring; it is a structural check against blind effects, deck quirks, and joker prerequisites.
skeleton.tip: Use Skeleton before you commit shop money on ante 1; a 30-second sanity check often saves the whole run.

decks.title: Decks
decks.body: Every deck with its starting modifier, stake compatibility notes, and strategic hooks pulled from dood.gg deck guides. Click a deck for build archetypes that perform especially well with it.
decks.tip: Plasma and Abandoned have the biggest score-curve differences; the deck tab spells out the implications.

stakes.title: Stakes
stakes.body: All stakes from White to Gold, with the cumulative modifier each one adds. Includes a quick reference for which jokers are nerfed (Eternal/Perishable/Rented chance) and which run conditions tighten at higher stakes.
stakes.tip: When laddering stakes, the Black Stake (Perishable jokers) is the hardest mental shift; pair this tab with Tier List filtered by 'survives Black'.

bosses.title: Boss Blinds
bosses.body: Every boss blind, its disable condition, the antes it can appear at, and counter strategies. Tabs filter by ante range and effect type (suit ban, hand ban, scoring penalty, etc.).
bosses.tip: When My Run flags an upcoming boss, this tab opens directly to a per-boss counter card with recommended jokers.

vouchers.title: Vouchers
vouchers.body: Every voucher and its tier-2 upgrade, with shop pricing, prerequisites, and impact estimates. Vouchers are run-defining choices; this tab helps weigh them.
vouchers.tip: Sort by 'economic impact' to surface money-printing vouchers (Overstock, Reroll Surplus) first; sort by 'scaling' for build-enablers.

consumables.title: Consumables
consumables.body: Tarots, Planets, Spectrals, and the special cards. Each entry shows the exact effect, when it is most efficient (e.g. Planet cards level up the right hand type), and whether it is destroyed on use.
consumables.tip: Use the search bar with terms like 'enhancement', 'edition', or 'destroy' to find every consumable that touches a mechanic.

modifiers.title: Modifiers
modifiers.body: Card editions (Foil, Holographic, Polychrome, Negative) and enhancements (Bonus, Mult, Wild, Glass, Steel, Stone, Gold, Lucky). Stats, conflicts, and best pairings are listed for each.
modifiers.tip: Negative is the only edition that gives a joker slot; the modifiers tab makes the trade-off vs raw XMult explicit.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.body: Plain-English definitions for every gameplay term Balatropedia uses: chips, mult, XMult, retrigger, scoring sequence, blind, ante, etc. Useful when reading synergy notes that assume jargon.
glossary.tip: Glossary entries are linked from joker effect text via underlined wiki-style links; click to jump straight here.

favorites.title: Favorites
favorites.body: Everything you have starred (jokers, decks, vouchers, consumables, modifiers, bosses, stakes, archetypes, combos) collected in one tab. Filter by type to plan around just the ones you care about.
favorites.tip: Sign in with Google to keep favorites across devices; otherwise they live in this browser only.

whatsnew.title: What's New
whatsnew.body: The version-by-version changelog. New features, balance-data updates, fixes, and polish are categorized with icons. The home tab shows only the most recent entry; this tab shows them all.
whatsnew.tip: Bookmark this tab if you watched the project from earlier versions; the cumulative changelog is short and easy to skim.

about.title: About
about.body: Project goals, attribution, source guides, and the disclaimer that Balatropedia is fan-made and not affiliated with LocalThunk or Playstack. Links to the GitHub repository and the issue tracker live here.
about.tip: Found a wrong number or a missing joker? The 'Submit a correction' link opens a pre-filled GitHub issue.

settings.title: Settings
settings.body: Theme (dark/light), language (English / Français / Español), sound effects toggle, UI scale slider (80% to 160%), and account controls. The UI scale slider rescales joker sprites and key UI elements without breaking layout; useful on high-DPI desktops and large mobile screens.
settings.tip: If sprites feel too small on a 4K monitor, push UI scale to 130-140%. On phones, leave it at 100% unless you have specific accessibility needs.

kofi.footer_note: Tips help cover server costs and keep Balatropedia free for everyone.

kofi.tagline: If this tool helps you cook your runs, you can tip the dev a coffee. Donations go toward supporting me and upgrading the servers so more people can use the app without hiccups.
